title: Emberhold
aesthetic: heroic dark-fantasy — orchestral-leaning synth (brass, strings, harp, bell), warm against the cold
_comment: The MUSIC bible for the Emberhold world. It `extends` the Root Spec (worlds/emberhold/world.json) — the same world the sprite bible (groups/sprites/emberhold) extends, so both media share one identity. `ember_theme` is this Lab's FACE of the world's cross-modal `ember` motif: the melodic twin of the gold sun-crest sprite. `main-theme` states it in full (hope); `siege` is the world's `fallen` transform — the very same theme inverted and dragged into a minor dirge, the audio twin of the dark-knight palette-swap.

brass._comment: Heroic lead — bright detuned saws, resonant filter, a little delay for the hall.

strings._comment: Supersaw pad, slow swell + vibrato — the wall standing firm.

harp._comment: Karplus pluck for rolling arpeggios — the ember's flicker.

bell._comment: Inharmonic FM bell — the sacred flame glinting overhead.

bass._comment: Round triangle sub — felt more than heard.

ember_theme._comment: 8 beats / 2 bars in D minor. The FACE of the world's cross-modal `ember` motif (its sprite twin is the gold `crest`). A warm, rising hero hook — stated in full in main-theme, fragmented elsewhere. Its `fallen` transform (invert + octave down) is the siege dirge.

vanguard._comment: 4 beats. A march-like answer phrase (the world's `resolve` meaning) that carries continuity without restating the hook.

shadow_theme._comment: 8 beats / 2 bars in D minor. The FACE of the world's cross-modal `shadow` motif (its sprite twin is the broken `shadow_sigil`) — the fallen captain's own leitmotif, and the deliberate dark inverse of `ember_theme`: where the ember RISES through the triad (D->F->A) and settles home, the shadow FALLS from on high (A->F->D) and slides through the chromatic Ab (a tritone from D) and the C# leading tone — menace that never quite resolves upward. Stated in full in `boss`; fragmented as a warning elsewhere.
